item.title: I wish my bed would preheat
item.dc:creator: McKittyNuts

item.title: Things I saw on home inspections this month
item.description:
Hello friends, it's your friendly neighbourhood home inspector back with another dump of photos from this month. Business is still slow and will likely be so for at least another month so my weekly dumps will be monthly for now.
We are going to start this one off with a single kernel of forbidden popcorn. Vermiculite in Canada largely came from a single company which in turn got their stock from the Libby mine in Montana. Vermiculite from that mine was contaminated with asbestos which can cause cancer if inhaled. Without testing at a lab I can't say if this contains asbestos or not but I can say the cost of removal is high thousands on the low end. Not disturbing it is an option but then when it comes time to sell it can cause issues with the deal.
We found some old open wire plugs in the home which was to be expected as it predated 1962. If your home was built post 1962 and you have an open ground system the ground wire might just be loose or fallen off, or someone didn't do the job right. Either way call up an electrician.
This basement has flooded in the past and has been redrywalled. I'd be willing to bet there is missing insulation behind that drywall. The darker areas also had moisture content but the photo with the moisture meter was corrupted.
Some arcing or overheating occured on the bus bar. Call up an electrician to see if everything is kosher.
Not my best picture but a previous occupant had cut a hole and made their own duct out of a flexible hose.
So as a home inspector I'm not allowed to call mould mould. Instead it's an apparent biological growth.
Another thermal photo. Those thermal anomalies represent a leak from above. I also used a moisture meter here and it ranged from 66% to 89%. Mould growth can occur in 48 hours if the right conditions are met so hopefully the owner deals with this sooner rather than later.
Their downspout broke and is draining along the foundation.
I screwed up and don't have a picture of the other side. Do you see how the AC is unlevel? Yeah that's because the ground from the broken pipe is eroding the ground and causing more drainage issues.
Okay not the end of the worl-
Oh. This might need some help it shouldn't be crooked
This is the inside of an old single chamber septic that hasn't been pumped in a while. Mmm poop. Nowadays two chamber tanks are the norm and have been for a long time. While septics if properly cared for can in theory last a century those are the exception not the norm. Most installers will give a number between 20-40 years so this one is probably beyond that.
Another shot from outside. One of the main reasons two chambers are superior is to help prevent solids from flowing into your septic field and clogging it up. When you neglect to pump it and those solids go over the end of that T bad things can happen.
I can't even see the inlet pipe.
Some old two prong plugs which means they are almost certainly open ground.
Someone used some non-permanent shark bites on the main water near the shutoff.
Sylvania had some issues with the breakers (I think I could be misremembering the exact part with the issue it's early and I haven't had a coffee) which led to overheating. As such I always flag the name brand from a further investigation by an electrician.
Sure looks like there was some weeping down that chimney maybe the flashing was done. Hard to tell because you know snow.
Some water coming through the pad in the basement. I'd expect more come the spring.
Their furnace had a leak. An HVAC tech can find the fault and fix it.
See how the inside of that window is bowing onwards? More broken seals it's been a while since I posted about those.
It's hard to tell from this photo but the bottom right of that white "repair" was actually bowing outwards and in need of a mason to repair it correctly.
Someone's puppers was digging under the deck.
Thus ends another dump. If you are ever on Old Barry's Bay road go check out the old log flume. It's very pretty in the winter.
item.dc:creator: XeononSolomon

item.title: I'm seeing some complaints about a flag in the new Suicide Squad game. I thought I'd share a comic based on a thing Kevin Conroy wrote. "Finding Batman".
item.description: Some people don't know how good they have it if only a flag is something that angers them. Imagine living your life being told what you are is wrong and bad. Forced Heteronormity was (and is) shoved down queer people's throats every day. Some have to conform to survive Front Page Edit: Batman is cool.
item.dc:creator: murderhobbit

item.title: Lord of the Rings Wholesome (meme) dump
item.description:
1. Awww
2
3. He must be stopped!
4
5. Ask why Aragorn is overacting when kicks the helmet?
6
7. I get that playing golf might be fun, but I will never understand people that are WATCHING it. Also how come the prize money are so huge?
8
9
10
11
12
13
14. You should have said it!
15. And finally I would like to share with this awesome community my passion project. Me and my brother have started working games in the past few years. From scratch basically and we learned how to do it on our own. Today I'm proud to say our third game is close to release. It's inspired by the "The Stanley Parable" (same kinda of style and humor) and it would mean the world to us if you wishlisted the game on Steam. Check out the page here: https://store.steampowered.com/app/2336120/Do_Not_Press_The_Button_To_Delete_The_Multiverse/ Also we finally have a voice actor to narrate the game! : ) I wish you all success in your personal passion projects! It's a blast to work on one :)))
item.dc:creator: TheseusGames

Suggested Track Playlists: (HIT SHUFFLE) YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLoIzKPbKfwniKOaadw-tB8kaEQiUQ1Yp4 Spotify: https://open.spotify.com/playlist/6cqV52puia81vNnPHxcz1W?si=yoUknxpQQVql9E-NZRQ4 *TOGGLE MATURE CONTENT IN SETTINGS TO VIEW OUR POSTS* ICM SQUAD: @vazquezdaniel, @ohvalleyofplenty, @nichoDriver, @Dirgekins, @onewallpaperaweek, @thedownlowe, @TheForbiddenSemicolon, @bagoffishstix, @JayKillBam, @majkongskull, @DingoJ0nes, @HandsomePenguin, @MrJwinkyface, @Gorgoror, @ciadmillefailte, @kaeldra, @DerpyVaz ICM TIP JAR: https://www.patreon.com/InterwebChexMix Just to let you know, in no way, shape, or form do we take credit for any random art, comics, or photos featured in this collection, only for the daily effort involved in comprising the collections themselves. Like what we do and how we do it? Leave a tip. Or don't. Cool, either way. Stay True & Stay Tuned - Meta Pathos
item.dc:creator: MetaPathos

item.title: Fossil Fuel industry knew as early as 1954
item.description: I forgot to add in the link. My mistake. Thanks for the reminder! https://amp.theguardian.com/us-news/2024/jan/30/fossil-fuel-industry-air-pollution-fund-research-caltech-climate-change-denial
item.dc:creator: WheezyLex

item.title: Baby raccoons on my deck
item.description:
With my most recent raccoon post going Most Viral (https://imgur.com/gallery/sEM0xnq) here is a flashback to the time I came home to find 2 baby raccoons napping on my deck.
This is as close as I got to them. I left them alone after this for hours, but their mom never came back. Eventually, I sadly had to call animal control, and they went to a shelter nearby. Last I heard, they are both doing well. Note: I have since painted my deck.
item.dc:creator: TheVoidFrog

item.title: Smile For Wednesday My Dudes
item.description:
Forget the routine, the mundane strife, And act like you give two shits about life. Force a grin if you must to lighten our moods. And have a smile-filled Wednesday, my dudes!
Forget the routine, the mundane strife, And act like you give two shits about life. Force a grin if you must to lighten our moods. And have a smile-filled Wednesday, my dudes!

I do some reviews as a hobby, if you wanna check them, feel free to visit https://hypeimmune.wordpress.com/ and yes, I'm open to feedback! EDIT2: I don't know anything about the Donut Operator guy. I just like cats and found this funny!
